description: Skill created as .md file instead of directory/SKILL.md
root_cause: Wrong file format — skills need to be a directory containing SKILL.md, not a standalone .md file at the skills root
prevention: After creating any skill, verify ls ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md exists

description: Dockerfile CMD used 'python telegram_bot/run_bot.py' which broke sys.path for inter-package imports
root_cause: Running a script directly adds its parent directory to sys.path instead of CWD; sibling package imports fail because the project root is not on sys.path
fix_applied: Changed CMD to ['python', '-m', 'telegram_bot.run_bot'] so CWD is on sys.path and all package imports resolve correctly
prevention: After writing any Dockerfile CMD that invokes Python, check: does the script import from sibling packages? If yes, CMD must use python -m <package.module> not python path/to/script.py

description: bot.py called Orchestrator(run_id=..., feature=..., accounts_file=...) but actual signature is Orchestrator(candidate_apk, feature_description, accounts)
root_cause: Orchestrator was instantiated with wrong keyword arguments — used assumed kwargs instead of verifying the actual __init__ signature; accounts_file was passed as a path string instead of a loaded list
fix_applied: Updated bot.py to load accounts.json first, then call Orchestrator(candidate_apk=apk_path, feature_description=feature, accounts=accounts_list) matching the real signature
prevention: After writing any code that instantiates a class, run: python -c "import inspect; from module import Class; print(inspect.signature(Class.__init__))" to verify the signature before use

description: Railway Docker image was ~4GB (full Android SDK + emulator), crashing the free tier which has a 512MB RAM limit
root_cause: Single Dockerfile included Android SDK, emulator tools, and all heavy dependencies. Railway free tier cannot run images this large. The bot process only needs Python + telegram-bot + anthropic (~200MB).
fix_applied: Created separate Dockerfile.bot (~200MB) for the Telegram bot with minimal dependencies. Full Dockerfile kept for device-host emulator runs only.
prevention: Before deploying to any cloud free tier: (1) count base image size + apt packages + pip packages, (2) if estimate > 500MB flag it, (3) split into lightweight bot image vs heavy runner image

description: Deployment status was polled in main session loops (4x sleep+check turns) instead of using background monitoring
root_cause: Used synchronous sleep+poll pattern in main session, burning 4 conversation turns waiting for Railway deployment. Each poll consumed a turn with no useful output.
fix_applied: Replaced inline polling with run_in_background=True on a single monitoring command or a background Agent that reports when deployment reaches SUCCESS/FAILED
prevention: Any wait for an external async process (deployment, build, test suite) must use run_in_background=True. Never poll in a loop inside the main session.
